summary: The session focused on Network Level Authentication (NLA) and its implementation with Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP), including troubleshooting the "CredSSP encryption oracle not available" error and setting up LDAPS using an existing domain-joined certificate authority. The user inquired about ensuring system support for NLA and how LDAPS functions during user logins on domain-joined clients.
facts: NLA is a security feature of RDP that requires authentication before establishing a remote desktop session. The user encountered an error stating that a CredSSP encryption oracle was not available while trying to connect to a remote system. The remote system the user is connecting to is running Windows Server 2019. The user asked how to set up LDAPS using an existing certificate authority that is domain-joined. LDAPS works by performing a secure LDAP bind operation to the Domain Controller using SSL to validate user credentials during login.
